title: Ram temple donation row: How counting room key became central to theft probe
content: Ayodhya police are intensifying their probe into the Ram Temple donation theft, planning to question accused Ramshankar Yadav, alias Tinnu, in jail. Fresh disclosures suggest Tinnu had a key to the donation counting room, potentially enabling him to manipulate cash offerings. Investigators are also examining claims of unrestricted access to administrative areas due to proximity to a former trustee, and how accused coordinated movements to evade CCTV surveillance during counting.

title: Fresh violence in Manipur as 20 houses in 3 villages set ablaze
content: Fresh violence erupted in Manipur's Kamjong district as unidentified attackers torched over 20 houses across three villages. Phaimol, a Kuki village, was set ablaze, followed by suspected retaliatory attacks on Tangkhul Naga villages. While security forces intervened, several structures were damaged. Investigations are underway to identify the perpetrators, with a Kuki group condemning the attacks and alleging involvement of armed cadres.

title: Tamil Nadu govt moves SC against Madras HC order banning cow slaughter
content: Tamil Nadu has approached the Supreme Court challenging the Madras High Court's sweeping ban on cow and calf slaughter. The state argues the High Court's order, based on a 1976 ruling, contradicts the Tamil Nadu Animal Preservation Act, 1958. This Act permits the slaughter of older, unfit cows under specific conditions, a provision the state contends was ignored by the blanket prohibition.

title: Balogun joins Zidane, Ronaldinho in unwanted World Cup history after red card vs Bosnia
content: USA secured a spot in the World Cup 2026 Round of 16 with a 2-0 win over Bosnia-Herzegovina. However, striker Folarin Balogun made unwanted history, becoming the fourth player to score and get sent off in a knockout match. Despite his red card for an accidental foul, the US team held on for the victory, but Balogun's suspension creates a selection headache for their next game against Belgium.

title: 'Que sera, sera': Overlooked in Punjab Cong revamp, Manish Tewari posts cryptic message
content: Congress MP Manish Tewari expressed his disappointment after being overlooked for new roles in Punjab's election committees.  He alluded to "insecurities" hindering talented individuals.  Despite this, Tewari acknowledged the party's long-standing support and his own dedication.  The appointments, announced by the AICC, saw other prominent leaders like Charanjit Singh Channi and Vijay Inder Singla given key positions for the upcoming 2027 Assembly elections.

title: Cisco to roll out AI agents to entire workforce, says in-house AI is most efficient
content: Cisco is rolling out personalized AI agents to all 90,000 employees starting August, a significant corporate AI adoption. These agents will manage tasks and optimize model selection for efficiency and cost. The company is also investing in upskilling and sees AI as a major growth opportunity, with AI-related orders surging. Despite the potential, Cisco acknowledges the adoption process can be challenging.

title: Thailand: 11-year-old crashes truck into group of pilgrims; 8 monks killed
content: Tragedy struck in northeastern Thailand as at least eight monks on a pilgrimage were killed when an 11-year-old boy lost control of a pickup truck. The vehicle veered off the road, striking the group. Five monks died at the scene, with three more succumbing to their injuries at a hospital. Fourteen others were hospitalized following the devastating incident. Authorities are investigating the cause.

title: Bengaluru daycare incident: Whistleblower was fired after reporting abuse of toddlers
content: A whistleblower's report of alleged child abuse at a Bengaluru daycare, located on Capgemini's campus, has surfaced after disturbing videos emerged. Five nannies have been arrested following the complaint, which detailed toddlers being mistreated. Authorities are investigating the incident thoroughly, with police vowing no one will be spared. The case highlights concerns about safety norms in city daycares.

title: After Meta layoffs, Zuckerberg says firms should empower workers, not replace them
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg believes AI-driven job losses are exaggerated, advocating for tools that boost individual productivity. This comes as the tech giant laid off 8,000 employees, reassigning another 7,000 to AI projects, to offset significant investments in its AI "reboot." While Meta pours billions into AI infrastructure, Zuckerberg suggests a focus on human empowerment could create more jobs, a view contrasting with some industry leaders.

title: FIFA WC Golden Boot: Kane draws level with Haaland, closes in on Messi & Mbappé
content: Harry Kane's stunning late brace against Congo DR has ignited the FIFA World Cup 2026 Golden Boot race, propelling him to five goals, just one behind leaders Messi and Mbappé.  Kane's heroics secured England's comeback win and kept their World Cup dreams alive, setting up an enthralling battle for the top scorer title as the knockout stages commence. He also surpassed Pelé on the all-time World Cup scoring list.

title: Fired for taking tea, biscuits from office: Jharkhand HC orders peon’s reinstatement
content: A Jharkhand court has ordered the reinstatement of a peon sacked for taking office tea and biscuits home. The court deemed the dismissal "grossly disproportionate," highlighting a vague show-cause notice and the lack of consideration for his plea. The bench emphasized that such a harsh penalty for a low-paid employee was insensitive, directing his re-employment with partial back wages.

title: Sam Altman wants US govt to own 5% of OpenAI; urges Google, Anthropic to follow
content: OpenAI's Sam Altman reportedly proposed giving the US government a 5% stake in the company, alongside similar stakes from other AI giants like Google and Meta. This initiative aims to ease political tensions and broadly share AI's economic benefits, drawing parallels to Alaska's oil wealth fund. The move comes amid increasing government scrutiny of AI's impact on jobs and cybersecurity.

title: '1st he cheats, then calls': Old clip shows Siya in heated call at nightclub
content: An old video of Siya Goyal, accused in her fiancé Ketan Agarwal's murder, has resurfaced, showing her in a nightclub using abusive language. Another clip shows her at a family function weeks before the alleged crime. Investigators are now scrutinizing Goyal's handling of Agarwal's mobile phone post-death, suspecting evidence tampering. The couple allegedly planned the murder, with a crime scene recreation underway.

title: Watch: Yashasvi Jaiswal flaunts 'Messi' jersey during France vs Sweden WC match
content: Dreams do come true! Cricket star Yashasvi Jaiswal celebrated a major milestone by attending the exciting France vs. Sweden FIFA match in New York, wearing an Argentina jersey, in honor of Lionel Messi. This appearance arrives as he takes a breather following an unusual absence from the most recent ODI selections, with eyes set on rejoining the Test series against New Zealand.

title: Monsoon arrives in India: Heavy rain lashes Delhi, Mumbai; to spread to northern, western states
content: Delhi finally welcomed the southwest monsoon on Thursday, five days past its usual arrival, bringing much-needed relief from heat. The monsoon is now widespread across northwest India, with heavy rains expected in Maharashtra, Goa, and Gujarat. This marks the first July onset for Delhi since 2021, as the monsoon rapidly advances, nearing full country coverage ahead of schedule.

title: New EPF scheme : What it means for your provident fund savings
content: India’s provident fund framework has now entered a new phase with the notification of the Employees’ Provident Funds Scheme, 2026 (EPF Scheme, 2026) under the Code on Social Security, 2020.

title: US de-lists four Indian companies sanctioned for aiding Russia's military base
content: In a significant move, the US Treasury has lifted sanctions on four Indian companies that were previously accused of supplying advanced technologies to the Russian military. The firms include RRG Engineering Technologies and Lokesh Machines from Hyderabad, Galaxy Bearings from Ahmedabad, and Shaurya Aeronautics from New Delhi. However, the US department has not provided specific reasons for their delisting.

title: She was 'too fat', incapable of conceiving: Karnataka man strangles wife to death
content: A 29-year-old woman was allegedly murdered by her husband in Dharwad over dowry demands and taunts about her weight. Police have arrested the husband and three others in connection with the death of Priyanka Kamalakar. The victim's family reported continuous harassment, with the husband reportedly also humiliating her about her physique. An altercation at their residence allegedly led to the fatal strangulation.

title: Watch: Congo coach stunned after learning of father's death during press conference
content: Congo DR's World Cup dream ended in heartbreak as England secured a 2-1 victory. Minutes after the match, coach Sébastien Desabre received devastating news of his father's passing during a post-match press conference. The emotional announcement stunned reporters and brought the media interaction to an abrupt halt, overshadowing the team's historic tournament run.

title: Buying a home? Here's how women can save on stamp duty across states
content: In multiple Indian states, financial incentives aimed at women buying property, such as slashed stamp duties and preferential home loan rates, are making a significant impact. Enhanced by government housing efforts like PMAY, these programs not only enhance women's property ownership but also promote financial equality and inclusivity, paving the way for joint ownership and facilitating access to higher mortgage amounts to create a balanced real estate market.

title: 100+ prominent India, Pakistan voices appeal for 'confidence building measures'
content: Over 100 prominent Indian and Pakistani citizens have penned an open letter to Prime Ministers Narendra Modi and Shehbaz Sharif, urging them to restore peace and dialogue. The signatories propose confidence-building measures like reinstating full diplomatic ties and resuming normal visa services. They emphasize that continued hostility hinders the prosperity and secure future of millions of young people in both nations, stressing the need for cooperation over confrontation.

title: Hilarious! Djokovic pranks ball girl, leaves Wimbledon crowd in splits - Watch
content: Novak Djokovic entertained Wimbledon fans with a playful prank on a ball girl during his second-round match against Stefanos Tsitsipas before cruising to a 6-3, 6-4, 6-2 victory. The Serbian later apologised jokingly for the prank and said it helped him relax. Djokovic's win kept him on course for a record-equalling eighth Wimbledon title and a 25th Grand Slam crown.

title: 'Happy Birthday': Ahead of July 4, Trump says he delivered his promise on 'plummeting' oil prices
content: Former President Donald Trump announced that the Freedom Fuel Network will slash gas prices at 25 Philadelphia-area stations on July 3rd as a "Happy Birthday" gift ahead of America's 250th anniversary. He highlighted falling oil prices, urging other retailers to follow suit and linking the move to his economic agenda. Trump asserted that gas prices would soon reach pre-Iran excursion lows.

title: How submarines launch ballistic missiles without blowing themselves up
content: Submarine-launched ballistic missiles utilize an innovative 'cold launch' method, setting a new global standard for safety. This technique propels missiles via high-pressure gas, preventing the ignition of rocket engines inside the submarine. Originating from Cold War developments, it enables launches from underwater, significantly bolstering nuclear deterrence and the safety of vessels and crews. Modern SLBMs, like India's K-4, integrate this essential technology.

title: Bribes given for jobs in Ram temple? Cops probe role of trust member
content: Ayodhya police are investigating claims of bribery for appointments at the Ram temple. An arrested individual's statements have pointed towards a Trust member's alleged involvement in a recruitment racket, where nearly 125 employees may have paid for their positions. Investigators are scrutinizing financial records and properties linked to the Trust member, while also examining the roles of two other arrested individuals, who are reportedly relatives of the Trust member.

title: 2 days after giving birth, she travelled 200 km for exam, became TN's 1st tribal judge
content: V. Sripathy, a 23-year-old from a remote Tamil Nadu village, achieved a historic feat by clearing the civil judge examination just two days after giving birth. Battling limited infrastructure and societal expectations, her journey from a farmer's daughter to the first civil judge from her tribal community exemplifies extraordinary grit and the power of education. Her determination inspires countless young women, proving that dreams can overcome significant obstacles.

title: Mahua names 16 'BJP goons' over mob attack, alleges police watched as eggs hurled
content: Trinamool Congress MP Mahua Moitra alleged a mob attack by BJP workers at a party office in West Bengal's Nadia district. She identified 16 individuals, claiming police remained silent spectators for four hours as protesters hurled eggs and mud. Moitra was later escorted out by police. The BJP denied involvement, suggesting internal TMC conflict, while other opposition leaders condemned the incident and police inaction.

title: 'Sooryavanshi should’ve played in Ireland': Shastri questions Team India's decision
content: Ravi Shastri has criticised Team India's decision to keep 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi on the bench, saying the youngster should have made his debut during the Ireland series. Calling him an "X-factor," Shastri said Vaibhav's fearless approach could provide explosive starts and urged the team management to include him soon after his impressive IPL performances.

title: Has obtaining cop cover become 8th ritual for a valid marriage: HC
content: Madhya Pradesh High Court has cautioned lawyers against advising newlyweds to seek police protection without genuine threats, calling it a potential "eighth ritual" after marriage. The court's observation came during a hearing where a woman alleged an advocate charged Rs 40,000 for her marriage and promised police protection. The bench emphasized advocates shouldn't exploit young couples and directed an investigation into the institution arranging marriages.

title: Indian exporters see more enquiries from US, but not many orders - here’s why
content: During 2025-26, the United States remained India's second-largest trading partner, with merchandise exports to the country rising 0.92% year-on-year to $87.3 billion.

title: After losing home in Kashmir, family loses only child in Delhi hit-and-run
content: Parents of Sarthak Mattoo, killed in a Delhi hit-and-run, have released an emotional video alleging police inaction and questioning the investigation. Their only child was struck by a Mahindra Thar on June 25, with the SUV allegedly fleeing the scene. The family highlights concerns over delayed blood sample collection, potentially hindering alcohol detection, and pleads with top leaders for justice.

title: FIFA WC: Ronaldo shares a 5-word message for Portugal fans ahead of R32 clash
content: Cristiano Ronaldo urged Portugal fans to "make us feel at home" ahead of their FIFA World Cup Round of 32 clash against Croatia. The match coincides with the first death anniversary of former Portugal striker Diogo Jota. Ronaldo, who recently became Portugal's all-time leading World Cup scorer, will aim to lead his side past Luka Modric and Croatia.

title: Why RBI’s new NBFC rules may force Tata Sons into the market
content: The Reserve Bank of India's updated master directions have effectively thwarted Tata Sons' bid to escape mandatory listing. New rules, particularly on indirect public funds and overseas investments, block the holding company's transition to an unregulated core investment entity. This move tightens RBI's oversight, focusing on economic substance over legal form, leaving Tata Sons with limited options.

title: 'Jana Nayagan' clears censor: Vijay's final film likely to hit theatres this month
content: Vijay's final film, 'Jana Nayagan,' has reportedly cleared its censor hurdle, receiving an 'A' certificate. This significant development resolves the major issue that delayed its release. Fans are eagerly anticipating an official announcement for the theatrical debut, with expectations high for the film's arrival later this month. Directed by H Vinoth, it features a star-studded cast and music by Anirudh Ravichander.

title: Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi meets PM Modi, receives ceremonial welcome at Rashtrapati Bhawan
content: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi received a ceremonial welcome at Rashtrapati Bhavan today, marking the start of her three-day official visit to India. She will attend the 16th India-Japan Annual Summit with Prime Minister Narendra Modi. This visit is poised to strengthen their Special Strategic and Global Partnership, focusing on areas like economic security, technology, and defense cooperation.

title: Why Iran fears another funeral disaster as Khamenei awaits burial for 4 months
content: Iran is meticulously planning Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's funeral to prevent the deadly crowd surges that marred previous burials. Lessons learned from the chaotic funerals of Ayatollah Khomeini and General Qasem Soleimani are shaping extensive security measures, including potential helicopter deployment and cross-border coordination with Iraq. The aim is to ensure Khamenei's final rites are a display of state control, not a repeat of past tragedies.

title: 'Optional, protected, not searchable': WhatsApp answers FAQs on 'Username' feature
content: WhatsApp is clarifying its upcoming username feature amidst government concerns about potential fraud and impersonation. The company assures users usernames are optional and will not be publicly searchable.  Existing popular names are reserved for legitimate owners.  However, the Indian government has asked Meta to halt the rollout until consultations are complete, citing risks of scams and identity spoofing.

title: Second-fastest man ever after Bolt! Why Jacobs' sprint won't enter record books
content: Olympic champion Marcell Jacobs blazed to a 9.67-second 100m at the Austrian Open, a time second only to Usain Bolt's world record. However, a strong tailwind of +4.1m/s means this incredible feat won't be officially recognised. Despite the wind, Jacobs' performance signals a strong comeback, placing him among the fastest wind-assisted runners ever.

title: Sergey Brin sold his NYC real estate: Here's what Mamdani's rent freeze cost him
content: Google co-founder Sergey Brin has divested his stake in a New York City real estate fund for a fraction of its value, selling for just six cents on the dollar. This significant loss comes as Mayor Zohran Mamdani's rent freeze tightens conditions for landlords. The fund, A&E Real Estate, confirmed the sale, citing the challenging market for rent-stabilized apartments.

title: Eggs thrown at Mahua as 'BJP men' hold her 'hostage': How 4-hour 'siege' unfolded
content: TMC MP Mahua Moitra was targeted with eggs and held for hours by alleged BJP supporters in Nadia, with police presence questioned. Meanwhile, Rajya Sabha MP Dola Sen faced black-flag protests in Durgapur. These incidents have drawn sharp criticism from TMC leaders, raising concerns about political intimidation and police action.

title: Lukaku plays peacemaker as Belgium teammates clash before stunning comeback vs Senegal
content: Belgium staged a sensational World Cup comeback against Senegal, winning 3-2 in a dramatic Round of 32 clash. Trailing 0-2 until the 86th minute, striker Romelu Lukaku first diffused an on-field spat between teammates before igniting the revival.  He scored, then Trossard assisted Tielemans for the equaliser, who later netted a record-breaking 125th-minute penalty to seal the victory after a lengthy VAR review.

title: Demography panel to study population changes post 2011 Census, prepares detailed questionnaire for States
content: The High-Level Committee on Demographic Changes will seek details of population changes and increase in settlements since the 2011 Census; it will also ask the ECI for data on SIR deletions

title: Three fans of actor Darshan held for threatening witness in Renukaswamy murder case
content: Police said one of the accused is the owner of the SUV that was allegedly used to transport Renukaswamy’s body from a shed in R.R. Nagar to a stormwater drain at Kamakshipalya, and also a witness in the murder case

title: Adani Enterprises, UAE’s IHC to invest ₹1.08 lakh crore in Odisha aluminium project
content: The project will be developed in two phases, with an investment of approximately ₹66,000 crore in Phase I and ₹44,000 crore in Phase II

title: A terrorist, is a terrorist: India calls for collective action to root out murderous ideology
content: Irrespective of any grievance, political cause or strategic calculation, terrorism in all its forms and manifestations must be condemned unequivocally, says India’s Permanent Representative to the UN Ambassador Harish Parvathaneni.

title: ‘Jana Nayagan’ leak case: 1.2 crore people watched Vijay-starrer online, Chennai police tell Madras High Court
content: Justice C. Kumarappan dismisses the bail petitions filed by two of the 21 accused after being told that they have been involved in a serious crime involving online leak of an uncertified movie

title: The Hindu Group’s Chairman, Director call on Tamil Nadu CM Vijay
content: Chairman N. Murali congratulated the Chief Minister on the TVK’s historic victory in the 2026 Assembly elections

title: How is Apple affected by a data breach at Tata Electronics? | Explained
content: Tata is reportedly beefing up its security measures and organising a forensic audit after a cybersecurity incident involving data leaks related to clients Apple and Tesla

title: AGICL invites architectural firms to empanel for nearly 100 Amaravati capital projects
content: The document states that Amaravati is being developed as a world-class capital city, with nearly 100 infrastructure projects worth over ₹56,000 crore already under implementation

title: ‘Jana Nayagan’ gets CBFC nod after long delay, official says
content: The film’s release, originally slated for January, may finally be released at some point following the issue of a certificate in the coming days

title: Former AIADMK Minister C. Vijayabaskar, senior leaders join TVK
content: Former Transport Minister M.R. Vijayabhaskar also joins party as exodus from the Dravidian major continues

title: Over 1,000 apply for Jana Sena posts in Vizianagaram ahead of local body polls
content: Ahead of the municipal and local body elections, the party has invited applications for committee posts across the district; most of the 1,000-plus applicants are aged 25 to 45

title: The case for equitable development in Tamil Nadu should apply to airport too: Poovulagin Nanbargal
content: Supporters of the Parandur airport argue that if Chennai does not get a second airport by 2028, the city and its surrounding industrial regions may lose future growth opportunities; the question, however, is whether such growth should be concentrated only around Chennai

title: Shortage of leukocyte filter sets continue to haunt thalassemia patients in Kozhikode
content: The supply of leukocyte filter sets under the ‘Ashadhara’ scheme was stopped last year after patients complained of adverse reactions

title: Why charge sheet in Pandit Sarla Bhat’s killing a milestone for J&K police? | Explained
content: The killing of the 27-year-old Pandit nurse was among the first cases of targeted killing of Kashmiri Pandits in the Valley following the armed insurrection in 1989; the filing of the charge sheet is seen by the family and the entire Pandit community as a step towards justice

title: Madras High Court dismisses ’Savukku’ Shankar’s plea for a CBI probe against former Chennai Police Commissioner Arun
content: Chief Justice Sushrut Arvind Dharmadhikari and Justice G. Arul Murugan say court’s jurisdiction must not be abused by individuals who pretend to fight for justice while acting on personal or hidden motives

title: Bombay civil court dismisses Gurbir Singh's plea against Samar Khadas, imposes costs
content: The dispute originated from a meeting held at the club premises, where Mr. Singh hosted a gathering that included individuals accused in the Bhima Koregaon case and facing prosecution under the UAPA

title: Telangan CM Revanth to visit Midjil, where his political journey began two decades ago
content: The visit is being billed not as a routine government programme but as a thanksgiving gesture to the people who first elected him and launched a political journey

title: YSRCP claims Rayalaseema Steel Plant as its work, cadres stopped at Kadapa site
content: The delegation, led by former Minister Sake Sailajanath, tried to march to the site two days before its construction begins; the party says the project is its legacy

title: GST at 9 — Revenues up 14% in June 2026 but dependence on imports increased, domestic sales lagged
content: As GST marked nine years of implementation on July 1, tax experts point to several aspects — such as input tax credits, dispute resolution, multiple registrations, and the inverted duty structure — as issues that still need to be addressed

title: Raja Raghuvanshi murder: Meghalaya police move Supreme Court against Sonam Raghuvanshi’s bail
content: The Meghalaya High Court, on June 29, had upheld an order of a Shillong trial court granting bail to Ms. Raghuvanshi on the ground that the police had failed to effectively communicate the grounds of her arrest

title: Arunachal clan launches drive to conserve endangered ray-finned fish, sustain tradition
content: Members of the Sangno clan translocate 52 fingerlings of the Schizothorax pelzami from one stream in East Kameng district to another, where they will be relatively safer from the predatory mahseer

title: Museum, water sports, resorts planned at Polavaram, says Nimmala Ramanaidu
content: The government is evaluating conceptual designs prepared by a Japanese consultancy for developing a tourism corridor from the Polavaram project to Bhadrachalam, via the Papikonda region, says Water Resources Minister Nimmala Ramanaidu

title: From rural Tamil Nadu to Wall Street: Chef Vijay Kumar on ringing the Nasdaq closing bell
content: In this exclusive essay for The Hindu, Vijay Kumar, the chef behind New York’s Michelin-starred Semma, reflects on his journey from a village near Natham in Tamil Nadu to global recognition, and what ringing the Nasdaq closing bell meant to him and the future of Tamil cuisine

title: Congress-BRS confrontation escalates into showdown in Hyderabad; alleged scam at Telangana’s Gurukuls under spotlight
content: Leaders challenged each other to public debates over allegations of irregularities in the procurement of uniforms, shoes and school bags for students of Telangana’s residential educational institutions (Gurkulams)

title: Nellore will be a growth engine for wealth creation, says Chandrababu Naidu
content: BPCL is investing nearly ₹1 lakh crore in a refinery at Ramayapatnam, while large-scale industries are expected at IFFCO Kisan SEZ, says the Chief Minister

title: How does WhatsApp’s username feature compare to Telegram’s? | Explained
content: Government said that the feature could “facilitate impersonation and identity spoofing, including impersonation by permitting the adoption of usernames closely resembling those of genuine persons or institutions”

title: Treasurer being shielded in Ram temple donation 'theft': Swami Avimukteshwaranand
content: Swami Avimukteshwaranand Saraswati questioned the composition of the temple trust, alleging that those who had fought the legal battle for the Ram temple were sidelined.

title: First robot-assisted total knee replacement surgery in govt hospital in Karnataka, boost to State’s healthcare system
content: Karnataka Medical College and Research Institute in Hubballi has become the first government hospital in the State to perform this procedure, sets a new benchmark for advanced orthopaedic care in State’s public health sector

title: PM Modi marching towards ‘environmental disaster’ in Great Nicobar: Congress
content: The nation's ecological conscience is on trial, says Congress leader Jairam Ramesh while raising concerns over the Great Nicobar Project.

title: BJP, RSS leaders looted donations received at Ram Temple, alleges KPCC chief
content: Congress would have no objection to the RSS if it accepted the Constitution, the tricolour, democracy and Mahatma Gandhi’s philosophy of equal respect for all religions, KPCC chief

title: Bengaluru special court rejects Priyank Kharge’s claim that RSS member can’t file defamation case as it is not a registered organisation
content: Various courts have declared that ‘the RSS is a definite, determinate, and identifiable body or class of persons; and consequently, where defamatory imputations are made against the RSS as an organisation, an individual member of the RSS is competent to maintain a complaint for defamation’

title: Where does your favourite Cyberabad restaurant rank? Check hygiene scores of 39 food establishments released by CMC
content: Cyberabad Municipal Corporation’s food safety teams assessed hygiene standards, food handling practices, pest control measures, storage conditions, labelling, record maintenance and compliance with FSSAI norms

title: AAP delivers final election promise with direct financial aid to women in Punjab
content: Women who register later will also receive Satkar Raashi with effect from July 1: Bhagwant Singh Mann

title: NIA cites larger conspiracy in Malappuram explosives seizure; court denies accused anticipatory bail
content: NIA is investigating seizure of 89,600 gelatin sticks and 1.05 lakh non-electric detonators from a lorry carrying onion sacks at Tirurangadi in Malappuram this February

title: Madras High Court refuses to quash case against Ponmudy for 2025 derogatory speech
content: Justice G.K. Ilanthiraiyan dismisses a criminal revision petition filed by the former Minister against the complaint having been taken cognisance of by a metropolitan magistrate

title: Seven killed as boulder collapses at stone quarry on outskirts of Bengaluru, death toll likely to rise
content: Police said several labourers were reported missing, raising fears that the death toll could increase

title: Kerala govt begins examining Adani proposal to sell 49% stake in Vizhinjam port to MSC Group
content: Announcement by Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone Limited has sparked a controversy in Kerala, with Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan claiming Adani Group had not communicated its decision to State govt and that its approval is mandatory for such a move

title: HSBC to set up integrated campus in Bengaluru
content: India is one of the most important talent centres in the world and continues to be a key market for HSBC: Georges Elhedery, group CEO, HSBC

title: Foreign medical graduates allowed to take up CRMI in 36 non-teaching hospitals in Tamil Nadu
content: This has fulfilled a long-pending demand of hundreds of FMGs, who had passed the Foreign Medical Graduate Examination and were waiting for the mandatory CRMI

title: Safe footpath campaign in Bengaluru receives an unexpected Norwegian push
content: A group of senior officials from the Norwegian Embassy undertake a run down the footpaths of Bengaluru, shares a video extolling the experience

title: Bengaluru college student alleges private photos leaked online, threatened with AI deepfake videos
content: She stated that the photographs had been shared in confidence with modelling agents between 2023 and 2024 when she was pursuing modelling opportunities, but they were never intended for publication or circulation

title: Four Nepalese nationals arrested after street brawl outside Vidhana Soudha in Bengaluru goes viral
content: A verbal altercation broke out between two groups after one group allegedly objected to the other causing a disturbance during a photo session

title: Bengaluru: FIR against crèche staff over claims of physical abuse and cruelty towards children
content: The alleged incidents took place on June 25 at the ‘Little Buds Day Care’ facility located within the Capgemini campus at Brookefield

title: Registration not enough to validate Hindu marriage if rituals, ceremonies not performed: Gujarat HC
content: Ceremonies like saptapadi provide a marriage with the spiritual, social, and legal status as a sacrament and samskara, the court said

title: Validity of law imposing restrictions on alienation of private school properties challenged before Madras High Court
content: Chief Justice’s Bench admits the plea challenging Section 30 of the Tamil Nadu Private Schools (Regulation) Act, 2019 and seeks State government’s response

title: Iran invites Congress president Kharge, former EAM Salman Khurshid, Pawan Khera for Khamenei funeral
content: The Indian delegation will be among a large group of foreign dignitaries that have been invited by Tehran for the burial-related events, which will start from July 3 and will continue till July 9

title: AIADMK’s rebels express unwillingness to accept new positions in the party
content: Asked whether they would switch over to the TVK or the DMK, they emphatically said they would not leave the organisation, and clarified that they were not against the party leadership

title: Odisha to spend ₹5,467 crore for free education from KG to PG over next five years
content: Aimed at making quality education more accessible, equitable and affordable for students, the scheme says eligible students admitted to regular mode courses in government and government-aided institutions will be provided complete waiver of admission fees.

title: Census 2027: Enumerators begin door-to-door visits for Houselisting phase in Kerala
content: More than 61,000 enumerators, mostly teachers and other government employees, have been deployed for the exercise. The State has been divided into houselisting blocks for data collection

title: Lie Detector Test For Siya Goyal, Lover Chetan In Fiance Ketan Agarwal's Murder Case
content: Agarwal was allegedly pushed to death from a cliff at Lohagad Fort in Pune by Goyal and Chaudhary. Both the accused are under police custody until July 3 as the investigation continues.

title: Kashmir Faces Mutton Crisis Over Punjab 'Transit Fee' Row Amid Wedding Season
content: Mutton dealers allege the contractors on Punjab highways impose a transit fee of Rs 20,000-25,000 on each truck carrying the livestock.

title: Before Fiance Ketan's Murder, A 'Push Off Cliff' Rehearsal By Siya, Lover
content: The Pune police have confirmed that Siya Goyal and Chetan Chaudhary practiced how to push Ketan off a cliff on a hill in Pune's Lulla Nagar.

title: 7 Killed, 5 Injured As Boulder Collapses In Bengaluru Stone Quarry
content: The incident took place when excavation work was underway using an excavator at the top of the quarry owned by Kaveri Company.

title: China's Critical Minerals Dominance A Threat To India, Japan? What Minister Told NDTV
content: Piyush Goyal recalled that during the COVID-19 pandemic, China had shut its border, causing "significant difficulty" to the supply chains around the world.

title: 5 Hospitalised After Bear Spray Discharged Accidentally At Japan Post Office
content: Amid a historic surge in deadly bear attacks across Japan, five post office visitors required hospital treatment after a canister accidentally discharged.

title: Microsoft, Tata Among Firms Planning India-Southeast Asia Submarine Cable
content: Microsoft, Lightstorm and telecom majors plan a 3,600 km undersea cable linking India, Singapore and Malaysia to power AI and cloud growth by 2029

title: Trump Taps Harvard Astronomer Avi Loeb To Lead His New UFO Council
content: Led by Avi Loeb, a new Trump administration scientific council is reviewing over 50 Pentagon records to evaluate UAP security risks.

title: India-Bound Oil Tanker Survives Gunfire Near Hormuz, Reaches Odisha Port
content: To ensure the safety of the vessel and its crew, the ship reportedly altered its route after the incident and continued its voyage towards the Indian coast without any further security breach.

title: China's Z.AI Is Here: Should India Worry About The Next AI Power Shift?
content: Powerful open AI models creates a cybersecurity problem.Modern AI systems can identify software vulnerabilities far faster than human security teams.

title: Heat Dome Brings Extreme Temperatures To US Ahead Of July 4 Celebrations
content: Dangerous 100-degree Fahrenheit heat domes trigger health warnings and open cooling centers across the Northeast during July 4th celebrations.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Why India Should Be Very, Very Alarmed About China's Teesta Move In Bangladesh
content: China had been angling for the project for years but was thwarted by the Hasina government's caution. That's no more the case now.

title: Crypto, Golf Clubs, Watches: How Trump Built $1 Billion Fortune Last Year
content: Trump's financial disclosure reveals $1.2 billion in crypto earnings last year, surpassing his real estate empire built over decades

title: Question Us, But Don't Abuse': Pawan Kalyan Warns Against Social Media Misuse
content: In a video message released, Pawan Kalyan said some people were misusing social media platforms and freedom of speech to target others.

title: Searching For Suu Kyi In Myanmar's Capital Of Confusion
content: Myanmar's Suu Kyi is under house arrest in Naypyidaw, but even generals don't know her exact location in the secretive, maze-like capital

title: Monsoon, Five Days Late, Arrives In Delhi
content: The India Meteorological Department, or IMD, said it is for the first time since 2021 that monsoon has arrived in July.
